objections.claim: The proposal does not address that the current dedup path is already using `K.kvList` with the wrong shape. `buildKernelInterface()` exposes `kvList` as the raw `kernel.kv.list(opts)` result (`kernel.js:447-466`), and other working callers iterate `list.keys` (`userspace.js:15-21`, `userspace.js:25-32`, `userspace.js:779-782`). The dedup block instead reads `recentKeys.length` and `recentKeys[recentKeys.length - 1]` (`userspace.js:606-608`), so simply changing `limit: 1` to `limit: 5` will not actually inspect five experiences unless the result-shape bug is fixed first.
objections.falsification_test: Change only the proposed constants (`limit: 5`, similarity `0.92`, salience tolerance) and replay two near-identical salient experiences with embeddings present. If `experience_deduplicated` still never fires and the second `experience:*` write still happens, this objection is confirmed; if dedup starts working without changing `recentKeys.keys`, it is false.

objections.claim: The proposal assumes recency that the repo code does not establish. Experience keys are timestamp-based (`userspace.js:590`, `userspace.js:653`), but `kvList` is a direct pass-through to `kernel.kv.list(opts)` (`kernel.js:465`), and the dedup site never sorts returned keys before picking comparison candidates (`userspace.js:606-608`). Elsewhere, when recency matters, the code sorts explicitly (`userspace.js:779-782`). Unless the implementation sorts `result.keys` by key name or timestamp first, 'last 5' may not actually mean newest five.
objections.falsification_test: Run the proposed logic against a stub `kv.list()` that returns the same five `experience:*` keys in non-chronological order. If the dedup comparison set or outcome changes with only the returned order changed, this objection is confirmed; if the implementation first normalizes to the newest five and stays stable, it is false.

objections.claim: Checking five candidates adds synchronous KV I/O on the experience write path. `writeMemory()` computes the embedding and then performs dedup before `kvWriteSafe()` (`userspace.js:574-628`), while `kvGet()` is a direct `this.kv.get()` wrapper (`kernel.js:2010-2021`). A straightforward last-5 implementation becomes one `kv.list()` plus up to five `kv.get()` round trips per salient embedded experience, all before the write completes. That can raise latency and read cost at scale unless it is benchmarked or batched.
objections.falsification_test: Benchmark a replay with many salient experiences under current code and under a last-5 implementation that performs five candidate reads serially. If end-to-end write latency, KV read count, and p95 session time stay effectively unchanged, this objection is false; if they increase materially, it is confirmed.

objections.claim: Lowering the similarity cutoff to `0.92` widens dedup around only the free-text embedding, not the full experience record. The embedding is built from `reviewRecord.observation || reviewRecord.narrative || ledger.final_text` (`userspace.js:578-581`), while the dedup guard ignores `pattern_delta` entirely (`userspace.js:163-175`, `userspace.js:598`) and only checks a scalar `affinity_magnitude` from desire alignment (`userspace.js:136-160`, `userspace.js:611-612`). Because `cosineSimilarity()` is plain vector cosine (`memory.js:7-17`), a `0.92` threshold can suppress experiences that are phrased similarly but differ in pattern surprise or in which desires they advanced or blocked.
objections.falsification_test: Construct two experiences whose embedding cosine lands between `0.92` and `0.95`, with nearly identical observation text but different `pattern_delta.scores` or different `top_positive`/`top_negative` desire sets that still yield the same `affinity_magnitude`. If the proposal deduplicates the second experience, this objection is confirmed; if it is retained, the objection is false.

objections.claim: A 10% salience tolerance weakens the one escalation signal the current rule intentionally preserves. Today the experience is skipped only when the new salience is not higher (`userspace.js:611`), and for `no_action` sessions that salience is already reduced to `evalResult.sigma` only (`userspace.js:512-523`). The stored salience then drives later episode ranking in `selectExperiences()` (`memory.js:64-76`). Treating a modest salience increase as 'close enough' can erase exactly the experiences whose new information is increased surprise or importance.
objections.falsification_test: Replay two otherwise near-identical `no_action` experiences where the later one raises `sigma` by less than 10%, for example from `0.55` to `0.60`, and then run `selectExperiences()` on the resulting history. If the proposal suppresses the later record and changes which experiences are eligible for reflection, this objection is confirmed; if the later record is still preserved, it is false.

objections.claim: Expanding the window from the most recent experience to any of the last five changes the semantics from adjacent-duplicate suppression to short-window recurrence suppression. The current comment explicitly scopes the check to 'the most recent experience' (`userspace.js:603-604`). With timestamp-based keys (`userspace.js:590`, `userspace.js:653`) and no guard for intervening cycles or state changes, an `A -> B -> A` sequence can lose the second `A` merely because the first `A` is still inside the five-record window, even though recurrence after an intervening event is itself informative.
objections.falsification_test: Create a three-step trace where experience `A1` is written, then a materially different `B`, then a similar-but-later `A2`, all within the last five stored experiences. If the proposal deduplicates `A2` against `A1`, this objection is confirmed; if it preserves `A2` because the intervening state change is accounted for, it is false.

overall_assessment: The proposal should not proceed as written. First fix the `kvList` result-shape mismatch and make candidate ordering explicit; otherwise 'last 5' is either a no-op or undefined. After that, benchmark the added KV reads and calibrate thresholds on replay traces, because `0.92` similarity plus salience tolerance broadens suppression while the current guards ignore `pattern_delta` and collapse desire alignment to a single norm.
